$comment: Central high-risk path defaults for the truthful-attribution-gate (the Truthful Attribution protocol §3). The ONLY thing the gate reads for high-risk classification is this file (exact globs, no prose) PLUS a repo's .github/gate-suite.json highRiskPaths, which may EXTEND but never remove these defaults (the gate verifies the effective set is a superset). A change matching ANY glob requires a tier=maintainer human Reviewed-by; the gate arm alone is rejected. Glob dialect: minimatch-style — `**` matches any number of path segments incl. /, `*` matches any chars except /, `?` matches one char except /. Paths are matched anchored full-path against added/modified/deleted/renamed files (both old and new path for renames). Parse failure of this file => the entire change is treated high-risk (fail closed). Removing a default requires editing this file — itself a high-risk path (org governance class), so maintainer-reviewed by construction. The five classes below are descriptive intent; the globs are normative.

classes.migrations: DB migrations, schema definitions, the extension-migration runner
classes.release-ci-infra: CI workflows, gate configs, CODEOWNERS, release/publish scripts — all repos
classes.org-governance: policy docs, gate-suite.json, high-risk-defaults.json, the attribution gate workflow itself
classes.extension-system-architecture: the extension SDK, loader, trust/signature gates, capability/transport registration, IoC audit gates
classes.docs-publish-pipeline: the docs.cinatra.ai build/deploy surface — the Starlight site config + content schema, the integrations registry (source of truth for what publishes to the live site), the docs sync compiler, and the integration docs-contract validator (cinatra-ai/ci#39 / docs#51 / ops#378)
